summary.title: Roadmap Persistence Gap Analysis
summary.focus: 'Orient-then-forget' pattern: prospective DAG edits not persisted to head.json
summary.methodology: Git history analysis + trail correlation + uncommitted change detection

suspiciousFastCommits.interpretation: Candidate DAG fix + immediate promotion (expected, not divergence)

suspiciousFastCommits.commits: fix 7 validators → fix remaining validators (1 min gap)
suspiciousFastCommits.interpretation: Rapid bugfix iterations (suggests prospective DAG work, but WAS committed)

persistencePatternDetection.definition: Orient updates the prospective roadmap but developer doesn't commit head.json → context clear loses changes
detectionStrategy: Look for: trail shows orient → DAG modifications but NO subsequent git commit
Pattern: multiple orient calls in session without corresponding head.json commits
Gap: prospective DAG in memory diverges from committed head.json on restart
persistencePatternDetection.findingsFromTrail: Trail entries show orient → complete → orient → complete sequencing, suggesting tight feedback loop. However, WITHOUT access to in-session memory state vs. git state, hard to detect divergence.

theoreticalVulnerability.scenario: Developer interacts with roadmap in memory (via orient, complete, etc.) without committing head.json changes
theoreticalVulnerability.evidence: 12 instances in last 24h where head.json commits are >30min apart BUT trail shows active roadmap activity
theoreticalVulnerability.gap: If developer closes terminal/clears context without `git add head.json`, prospective work is lost

examples.commit: roadmap: dispatch-system-001 complete, ready for mainline
examples.implication: Long idle period could indicate prospective DAG work in between without commit
Prospective DAG tweaking, then committed in batch (good!)

rootCauses: Prospective DAG edits happen in-memory (orient, show, evaluate)
Head.json only persists when explicitly committed
Context clear (Claude Code session reset) loses in-memory state
User may assume orient changes are auto-saved (they aren't)
No automatic head.json sync on context boundaries

incidenceEstimate.theoretical: ~5-10% of sessions likely have prospective work (based on idle gaps + active trail)
incidenceEstimate.cost: Lost roadmap structure, wasted local edits, confusion on restart

enforcementGaps: No warning: 'You have uncommitted roadmap changes'
No git hook to auto-commit head.json on orient (would break atomicity)
No state tracking: what changed in head.json this session?
